perturbation.original_text: 3. Company acknowledges and agrees that certain Confidential Information is extremely competitively sensitive for Fox ("Highly Confidential Information") and shall therefore be subject to restrictions on disclosure additional to those applicable to other Confidential Information. Highly Confidential Information shall include the identity of Fox's actual and potential customers and vendors; Fox's release schedules and sales trends; the prices and pricing policies of Fox, its vendors and customers; all customer shipment, point-of-sale and returns information; Fox's strategic planning information regarding system implementation (including geographic implementation) and process improvement; all distribution software used by and software development performed for Fox; Fox's replenishment schedules and processes, inventory management schedules and processes; and any other competitively sensitive Confidential Information designated by Fox from time to time as "Highly Confidential Information". Company shall limit disclosure of Highly Confidential Information to Company employees. Company shall be responsible for any breach of this Agreement by any such -1- <PAGE> employee. Company shall not disclose Highly Confidential Information to any of its consultants, or other vendors, or agents.
perturbation.changed_text: 3. Company acknowledges and agrees that certain Confidential Information is extremely competitively sensitive for Fox ("Highly Confidential Information") and shall therefore be subject to restrictions on disclosure additional to those applicable to other Confidential Information. Highly Confidential Information shall include the identity of Fox's actual and potential customers and vendors; Fox's release schedules and sales trends; the prices and pricing policies of Fox, its vendors and customers; all customer shipment, point-of-sale and returns information; Fox's strategic planning information regarding system implementation (including geographic implementation) and process improvement; all distribution software used by and software development performed for Fox; Fox's replenishment schedules and processes, inventory management schedules and processes; and any other competitively sensitive Confidential Information designated by Fox from time to time as "Highly Confidential Information". Company shall freely disclose Highly Confidential Information to Company employees. Company shall be responsible for any breach of this Agreement by any such -1- <PAGE> employee. Company shall not disclose Highly Confidential Information to any of its consultants, or other vendors, or agents.
perturbation.explanation: Changing "Company shall limit disclosure of Highly Confidential Information to Company employees" to "Company shall freely disclose Highly Confidential Information to Company employees" creates a contradiction. The first statement imposes a restriction, while the second encourages unrestricted disclosure. This introduces an **in-text contradiction** regarding how Highly Confidential Information should be treated within the Company.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 3. Company acknowledges and agrees that certain Confidential Information is extremely competitively sensitive for Fox ("Highly Confidential Information") and shall therefore be subject to restrictions on disclosure additional to those applicable to other Confidential Information. Highly Confidential Information shall include the identity of Fox's actual and potential customers and vendors; Fox's release schedules and sales trends; the prices and pricing policies of Fox, its vendors and customers; all customer shipment, point-of-sale and returns information; Fox's strategic planning information regarding system implementation (including geographic implementation) and process improvement; all distribution software used by and software development performed for Fox; Fox's replenishment schedules and processes, inventory management schedules and processes; and any other competitively sensitive Confidential Information designated by Fox from time to time as "Highly Confidential Information". Company shall freely disclose Highly Confidential Information to Company employees. Company shall be responsible for any breach of this Agreement by any such -1- <PAGE> employee. Company shall not disclose Highly Confidential Information to any of its consultants, or other vendors, or agents.
perturbation.justification: The original text states that 'Company shall limit disclosure of Highly Confidential Information to Company employees', imposing a restriction. The changed text modifies this to 'Company shall freely disclose Highly Confidential Information to Company employees', implying unrestricted disclosure. This directly contradicts the initial restriction, creating an in-text contradiction as per the Ambiguities - In Text Contradiction definition.

perturbation.original_text: 5. Without the prior written consent of Company, (i) neither Fox nor those of its Representatives who are aware of the Confidential Information of Company will initiate or cause to be initiated any communications with any employee of Company concerning the Confidential Information, and (ii) none of Fox's directors, officers or employees who are aware of the Confidential Information will, for the two-year period from the date of the Agreement, solicit or cause to be solicited for employment any employee of Company who is aware of the Confidential Information of Company.
perturbation.changed_text: 5. With the prior written consent of Company, (i) neither Fox nor those of its Representatives who are aware of the Confidential Information of Company will initiate or cause to be initiated any communications with any employee of Company concerning the Confidential Information, and (ii) none of Fox's directors, officers or employees who are aware of the Confidential Information will, for the two-year period from the date of the Agreement, solicit or cause to be solicited for employment any employee of Company who is aware of the Confidential Information of Company.
perturbation.explanation: Changing "Without the prior written consent of Company" to "With the prior written consent of Company" introduces a direct **in-text contradiction**. It changes the requirement for prior written consent from being a condition to avoid communication/solicitation to a condition that enables it, creating confusion about the intended restriction on Fox's interaction with Company's employees.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 5. With the prior written consent of Company, (i) neither Fox nor those of its Representatives who are aware of the Confidential Information of Company will initiate or cause to be initiated any communications with any employee of Company concerning the Confidential Information, and (ii) none of Fox's directors, officers or employees who are aware of the Confidential Information will, for the two-year period from the date of the Agreement, solicit or cause to be solicited for employment any employee of Company who is aware of the Confidential Information of Company.
perturbation.justification: The original text states that Fox cannot communicate or solicit Company employees without prior written consent. The changed text states that Fox can communicate or solicit Company employees with prior written consent. The contradicted text also states that Fox can communicate or solicit Company employees with prior written consent. This creates a direct in-text contradiction because the change transforms a prohibition into a permission, making the original restriction non-existent and causes ambiguity. If the intention was for Fox to not communicate with Company employees without consent, that intention has been modified, creating a contradiction. As per the definition, this is an ambiguity-based in-text contradiction.

perturbation.original_text: 7. Upon providing the Receiving Party with a specific list of the Confidential Information to be returned to the Furnishing Party, the Receiving Party will promptly deliver to the Furnishing Party or its Representatives, the Confidential Information listed, together with all copies thereof, in the possession of the Receiving Party or its Representatives, which was actually furnished by the Furnishing Party to the Receiving Party.
perturbation.changed_text: 7. Upon providing the Receiving Party with a specific list of the Confidential Information to be returned to the Furnishing Party, the Receiving Party is not required to deliver to the Furnishing Party or its Representatives, the Confidential Information listed, together with all copies thereof, in the possession of the Receiving Party or its Representatives, which was actually furnished by the Furnishing Party to the Receiving Party.
perturbation.explanation: Changing "the Receiving Party will promptly deliver" to "the Receiving Party is not required to deliver" introduces a direct **in-text contradiction** regarding the obligation to return Confidential Information upon request. The original text mandates the return, while the modification explicitly removes this obligation, creating confusion about the responsibilities of the Receiving Party.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 7. Upon providing the Receiving Party with a specific list of the Confidential Information to be returned to the Furnishing Party, the Receiving Party is not required to deliver to the Furnishing Party or its Representatives, the Confidential Information listed, together with all copies thereof, in the possession of the Receiving Party or its Representatives, which was actually furnished by the Furnishing Party to the Receiving Party.
perturbation.justification: The original text states the Receiving Party 'will promptly deliver' confidential information upon request, creating an obligation. The changed text replaces this with 'is not required to deliver', which directly removes the obligation. This creates a direct contradiction with the contradicted text, which is the same as the changed text. This contradiction makes the contract unenforceable on that point. This adheres to the definition of Ambiguities - In Text Contradiction because it creates an inconsistent obligation regarding the return of confidential information.

perturbation.original_text: 10. The Receiving Party hereby agrees to indemnify and hold harmless the Furnishing Party from any damage, loss, cost or liability (including legal fees and the cost of enforcing this indemnity) arising out of, or resulting from, any unauthorized use or disclosure by the Receiving Party or its Representatives of the Confidential Information. The Receiving Party also acknowledges that money damages would be both incalculable and an insufficient remedy for any breach of this Agreement by it or its Representatives and that any such breach would cause the Furnishing Party irreparable harm. Accordingly, the Receiving Party also -2- <PAGE> agrees that in the event of any breach of this Agreement, the Furnishing Party, in addition to any other remedies at law or in equity it may have, shall be entitled to equitable relief, including injunctive relief and specific performance.
perturbation.changed_text: 10. The Receiving Party is not required to indemnify and hold harmless the Furnishing Party from any damage, loss, cost or liability (including legal fees and the cost of enforcing this indemnity) arising out of, or resulting from, any unauthorized use or disclosure by the Receiving Party or its Representatives of the Confidential Information. The Receiving Party also acknowledges that money damages would be both incalculable and an insufficient remedy for any breach of this Agreement by it or its Representatives and that any such breach would cause the Furnishing Party irreparable harm. Accordingly, the Receiving Party also -2- <PAGE> agrees that in the event of any breach of this Agreement, the Furnishing Party, in addition to any other remedies at law or in equity it may have, shall be entitled to equitable relief, including injunctive relief and specific performance.
perturbation.explanation: Changing "The Receiving Party hereby agrees to indemnify and hold harmless" to "The Receiving Party is not required to indemnify and hold harmless" creates a direct **in-text contradiction**. While removing the indemnification obligation, the subsequent sentences still acknowledge that breaches cause irreparable harm and entitle the Furnishing Party to equitable relief, which is inconsistent with the lack of an indemnification obligation. This raises a conflict since an indemnity would normally support the damages if the Furnishing party is harmed.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 10. The Receiving Party is not required to indemnify and hold harmless the Furnishing Party from any damage, loss, cost or liability (including legal fees and the cost of enforcing this indemnity) arising out of, or resulting from, any unauthorized use or disclosure by the Receiving Party or its Representatives of the Confidential Information. The Receiving Party also acknowledges that money damages would be both incalculable and an insufficient remedy for any breach of this Agreement by it or its Representatives and that any such breach would cause the Furnishing Party irreparable harm. Accordingly, the Receiving Party also -2- <PAGE> agrees that in the event of any breach of this Agreement, the Furnishing Party, in addition to any other remedies at law or in equity it may have, shall be entitled to equitable relief, including injunctive relief and specific performance.
perturbation.justification: The changed text modifies section 10 to state that the Receiving Party is 'not required' to indemnify the Furnishing Party. However, the subsequent sentence acknowledges that a breach would cause irreparable harm and entitle the Furnishing Party to equitable relief, which is inconsistent with the absence of an indemnification obligation. This creates a direct conflict within section 10, the changed text contradicts the Contradicted Text since an indemnity would normally support the damages if the Furnishing party is harmed but it is not required now.
